code:
def generate_signals(df, params):
    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np
    import ta
    
    # Parameters
    fast_ma = params['fast_ma']
    slow_ma = params['slow_ma']
    atr_period = params['atr_period']
    atr_mult = params['atr_mult']
    
    # Calculate moving averages
    ma_fast = df['close'].rolling(window=fast_ma).mean()
    ma_slow = df['close'].rolling(window=slow_ma).mean()
    
    # Calculate ATR for filter
    atr_indicator = ta.volatility.AverageTrueRange(high=df['high'], low=df['low'], close=df['close'], window=atr_period)
    atr = atr_indicator.average_true_range()
    
    # Entry signals: crossover with ATR filter
    # Long: fast crosses above slow AND recent volatility is below threshold (trend is not chaotic)
    # Short: fast crosses below slow AND recent volatility is below threshold
    long_condition = (ma_fast > ma_slow) & (ma_fast.shift(1) <= ma_slow.shift(1)) & (atr < atr_mult * atr.rolling(20).mean())
    short_condition = (ma_fast < ma_slow) & (ma_fast.shift(1) >= ma_slow.shift(1)) & (atr < atr_mult * atr.rolling(20).mean())
    
    # Initialize position series
    position = pd.Series(0, index=df.index)
    
    # Track position and trailing stop
    current_position = 0
    entry_price = 0.0
    highest_price = 0.0
    lowest_price = 0.0
    bars_since_entry = 0
    max_bars = 20  # time exit after 20 bars
    
    for i in range(1, len(df)):
        if current_position == 0:
            if long_condition.iloc[i]:
                current_position = 1
                entry_price = df['close'].iloc[i]
                highest_price = entry_price
                lowest_price = entry_price
                bars_since_entry = 0
            elif short_condition.iloc[i]:
                current_position = -1
                entry_price = df['close'].iloc[i]
                highest_price = entry_price
                lowest_price = entry_price
                bars_since_entry = 0
        else:
            # Update highest/lowest for trailing stop
            if current_position == 1:
                highest_price = max(highest_price, df['close'].iloc[i])
                # ATR trailing stop: exit if close drops below highest - atr_mult * atr
                if df['close'].iloc[i] < highest_price - atr_mult * atr.iloc[i]:
                    current_position = 0
                # Time exit
                elif bars_since_entry >= max_bars:
                    current_position = 0
            else:  # short position
                lowest_price = min(lowest_price, df['close'].iloc[i])
                # ATR trailing stop: exit if close rises above lowest + atr_mult * atr
                if df['close'].iloc[i] > lowest_price + atr_mult * atr.iloc[i]:
                    current_position = 0
                # Time exit
                elif bars_since_entry >= max_bars:
                    current_position = 0
            
            # Check opposite signal for exit
            if current_position == 1 and short_condition.iloc[i]:
                current_position = 0
            elif current_position == -1 and long_condition.iloc[i]:
                current_position = 0
            
            bars_since_entry += 1
        
        position.iloc[i] = current_position
    
    return position.astype(int)
rationale: In EUR/JPY, moving average crossovers capture trending behavior, but false signals are frequent during high volatility; filtering entries with below-average ATR (normalized by its own 20-period mean) avoids choppy markets and lets the trend ride with an ATR trailing stop.
